title: Pixelfed
description: Free, federated (ActivityPub) photo-sharing platform - a self-hosted, ethical alternative to Instagram for the Fediverse.
installation: This template runs the PRIMARY Pixelfed web/php-fpm container only. The zknt/pixelfed image serves over HTTP on port 80 and ships a php-fpm + nginx supervisord bundle. REQUIRED first-run steps: 1) Generate an APP_KEY with 'php artisan key:generate' (or 'openssl rand -base64 32') and set it as 'base64:...' - rotate the placeholder before going live. 2) After the DB is up, exec into the pod and run 'php artisan migrate --force' and 'php artisan storage:link', then 'php artisan import:cities' and 'php artisan instance:actor' to seed the federation actor. 3) Create the first admin with 'php artisan user:create' (or register then 'php artisan user:admin <id>'). COMPANION SERVICES to deploy separately for a full instance (same zknt/pixelfed image, different command): a HORIZON QUEUE WORKER ('php artisan horizon') is MANDATORY for federation, media optimization, and notifications - deploy it as a second app pointing at the same MariaDB + Valkey; an optional SCHEDULER/cron loop ('php artisan schedule:work') for digests and cleanup. Pixelfed stores uploads/avatars under /var/www/storage (mounted here) - the worker pod must share or replicate this path (use RWX storage) so federated media is reachable. Object storage: for production at scale point FILESYSTEM_DRIVER + AWS_* / S3 vars at an external S3 bucket (e.g. the RustFS add-on) instead of the local volume; omit it here for a single-node start. Rotate the MariaDB password, Valkey is password-less in-cluster. Set ADMIN_DOMAIN / APP_DOMAIN to your real host before federating - it is baked into ActivityPub actor URLs and cannot be changed cleanly later.
